title: Cancel a paid order before shipment
stakeholder: Authenticated customer who owns the order
trigger: The customer selects Cancel order on an eligible order
success_outcome: The order is cancelled and one refund request is recorded
tldr: Cancel an owned PAID order and create a refund request

in: Cancel an owned order in PAID state

behavior: On POST /orders/{orderId}/cancel, verify ownership and PAID state, transition order to CANCELLED, and persist one RefundRequest keyed on the cancellation id.
business_rules:
- ORDER_NOT_OWNED: only the owning customer may cancel; reject non-owners with 403.
- ORDER_ALREADY_SHIPPED: cancel only PAID orders; reject SHIPPED orders with 409.
- ORDER_NOT_FOUND: reject unrecognised order ids with 404.
- At most one refund request per payment; reuse cancellation id as idempotency key.
acceptance_scenarios: Given an authenticated owner of a PAID order, when they cancel it, then status becomes CANCELLED and a refund request is created
Given a non-owner, when they cancel, then 403 ORDER_NOT_OWNED is returned
Given an owner of a SHIPPED order, when they cancel, then 409 ORDER_ALREADY_SHIPPED is returned
inputs_outputs_errors: Input: orderId (UUID, path param). Output: updated Order with status=CANCELLED. Errors: 404 ORDER_NOT_FOUND, 403 ORDER_NOT_OWNED, 409 ORDER_ALREADY_SHIPPED.
definition_of_done: All acceptance scenarios have passing tests
Errors ORDER_NOT_FOUND, ORDER_NOT_OWNED, ORDER_ALREADY_SHIPPED are returned with correct HTTP status codes
